title: Episode 3 — Who are you? Name, Origin, Job

lines.text: Welcome back to Adipoli German — On the Go. Episode three.
In this episode, you'll learn to introduce yourself fully. Name, where you're from, and what you do. Let's begin.
From the last episodes, how do you say hello, formally?
Guten Tag.
And how do you ask how someone is, formally?
Wie geht es Ihnen?
Sehr gut. Now let's go further.
To say 'my name is' in German, you say:
Ich heiße.
Repeat.
Ich heiße.
Now add your own name. If you are Kuttan, you'd say:
Ich heiße Kuttan.
Try it with your name now. Ich heiße — and your name.
To ask formally, 'what is your name?', you say:
Wie heißen Sie?
Repeat.
Wie heißen Sie?
Now, to say where you're from. The pattern is Ich komme aus — I come from.
Ich komme aus.
Followed by your place. Say:
Ich komme aus Kerala.
Say it with YOUR region now. Ich komme aus — and where you're from.
Now combine. Imagine someone asks: Wie heißen Sie und woher kommen Sie? What is your name and where are you from?
You might say:
Ich heiße Kuttan. Ich komme aus Kerala.
Try it with your details now.
Now, what do you do? The verb sein — to be — makes this easy. Ich bin — I am.
Ich bin.
If you're a student, you say:
Ich bin Student.
For women, add -in at the end:
Ich bin Studentin.
If you're a nurse:
Ich bin Krankenpfleger.
Or Krankenpflegerin, for a woman. A mouthful — but important.
Krankenpflegerin.
Say your own job. Ich bin — and your profession. If you don't know the German word yet, just say your job in English — Germans will understand.
Now the full combo. Imagine the Goethe A1 examiner asks: Stellen Sie sich bitte vor. Please introduce yourself.
You say, all together:
Ich heiße Kuttan. Ich komme aus Kerala. Ich bin Student.
Now you. Your name, your region, your job. All three sentences.
Adipoli machaa! That's a Goethe A1 pass-level self-introduction. Practice it all day today — in the mirror, on the train, while cooking.
In the next episode, we learn numbers zero through twenty, and how to ask for prices. Until then, bis bald!
Bis bald!
